page: Amazon Web Services d3en.6xlarge is a dense storage instance optimized for network and EBS throughput. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 24 vCPUs and 12 physical cores operating at 3.1 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key feature of this instance is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 167,760 GB of HDD storage. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. Single-core processing capabilities are weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng single-core, geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance is average, with middle-50% scores in stress-ng multi-core, geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks is average. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also perform within the average tier.

The primary tradeoff of this instance lies in its storage medium and compute distribution. While it offers an exceptionally high storage-to-compute ratio with over 167 TB of local capacity, the storage is HDD-based rather than SSD, which limits random IOPS compared to solid-state alternatives. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance provides high resource density for sequential storage capacity and network throughput, making it highly efficient for workloads that require massive data retention without requiring top-tier single-core compute. This profile is appropriate for large-scale data warehousing, log processing, distributed file systems, and massive sequential data storage workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services d3en.6xlarge is a dense storage instance designed for network and EBS-optimized workloads. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 24 vCPUs, 12 physical cores, and 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance is characterized by its massive 167,760 GB of local HDD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. Benchmark results show weak single-core CPU performance, but average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also fall into the average performance tier. The primary tradeoff is the reliance on HDD storage, which offers high capacity but lower random read/write speeds than SSDs. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density storage requirements, making it suitable for distributed file systems, data lakes, and large-scale sequential data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d3en.6xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 24 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, 167,760 GB of local HDD storage, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth for large-scale data workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services d3en.6xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 24 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 167,760 GB of local HDD storage.
tagline: A dense storage instance combining massive local HDD capacity with balanced memory and average multi-core processing capabilities.
bullet_points: Features 24 vCPUs and 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory on an Intel Xeon architecture
Equipped with 167,760 GB of local HDD storage for high-capacity data retention
Provides 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and Nitro hypervisor optimization
Delivers average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Suited for distributed file systems, data warehousing, and log processing
